meaning: adj.连续的,相续的,有继的,有顺序的,结果的
examples: Many chapters are, of course, inherently sequential in nature.
Moreover, an examination of the apparent sequential connections among military, economic, and cultural forms of progress might help explain the dynamics of historical change.
Still, it's the company's first sequential quarterly decline, down 3% from the fourth quarter of 2008.
This is likely to have a significant negative impact on its year-over-year and sequential revenue figures.
